Operation 'ListSubuserBrandedLink' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The description lacks clarity about required inputs (subuser ID parameter) and expected outputs (what the response contains). Added specific details about the required parameter and response format to improve AI agent interpretability for determining when and how to invoke this operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve the branded link associated with a subuser.**

Link branding can be associated with subusers from the parent account. This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's link branding. To associate link branding, the parent account must first create a branded link and then validate it. The parent may then associate that branded link with a subuser via the API or the [Subuser Management page of the Twilio SendGrid App](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/subusers).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the branded link associated with a specified subuser. Link branding allows subusers to send mail using their parent account's branded links. The parent account must first create and validate a branded link before associating it with a subuser. Requires the subuser ID as a query parameter. Returns the branded link configuration object for that subuser, or an error if no link is associated.
data.reasoning: The description lacks clarity about required inputs (subuser ID parameter) and expected outputs (what the response contains). Added specific details about the required parameter and response format to improve AI agent interpretability for determining when and how to invoke this operation.

message: Operation 'DisassociateBrandedLinkFromSubuser' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The description mentions the 204 response code but lacks clarity about required inputs (subuser ID parameter) and the specific effect of the operation. Enhanced description to explicitly state the required parameter, the consequence of the action (subuser loses branded link access), and clarified that only parent accounts can perform this operation for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Disassociate a branded link from a subuser
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to take a branded link away from a subuser.**

Link branding can be associated with subusers from the parent account. This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's link branding. To associate link branding, the parent account must first create a branded link and validate it. The parent may then associate that branded link with a subuser via the API or the [Subuser Management page of the Twilio SendGrid App](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/subusers).

Your request will receive a response with a 204 status code if the disassociation was successful.
data.description_suggestion: Removes the branded link association from a specified subuser. Requires the subuser ID as a query parameter. After disassociation, the subuser will no longer be able to use the parent account's branded link for sending emails. Returns a 204 No Content status code on successful disassociation. This operation is only available to parent accounts.
data.reasoning: The description mentions the 204 response code but lacks clarity about required inputs (subuser ID parameter) and the specific effect of the operation. Enhanced description to explicitly state the required parameter, the consequence of the action (subuser loses branded link access), and clarified that only parent accounts can perform this operation for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve all branded links**.

You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all branded links configured for your account. Supports pagination and filtering via query parameters. Returns an array of branded link objects containing domain configuration, validation status, and default link settings. You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about the 2 parameters available for this operation (likely pagination/filtering parameters) and what the response contains. Description mentions the 204 response and advises retrieving details first, but lacks critical side effect information about what happens after deletion (emails revert to default link handling) and that the action is permanent. Enhanced description clarifies the irreversible nature and downstream effects to help AI agents understand the full impact of this destructive operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to delete a branded link.**

Your request will receive a response with a 204 status code if the deletion was successful. The call does not return the link's details, so if you wish to record these make sure you call the  "Retrieve a branded link" endpoint *before* you request its deletion.

You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a branded link by its ID. Returns a 204 No Content status on successful deletion with no response body. Warning: This action cannot be undone and will remove all link branding configuration for the specified domain. Any emails using this branded link will revert to default SendGrid link handling. Retrieve the link details before deletion if you need to preserve them. You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.reasoning: Description mentions the 204 response and advises retrieving details first, but lacks critical side effect information about what happens after deletion (emails revert to default link handling) and that the action is permanent. Enhanced description clarifies the irreversible nature and downstream effects to help AI agents understand the full impact of this destructive operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to validate a branded link.**

You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.description_suggestion: Validates a branded link by verifying its DNS configuration and domain ownership. Checks that all required DNS records are properly configured for the branded link to function correctly. Returns validation status and details about any configuration issues found. A branded link must be validated before it can be associated with subusers or used for email delivery. You can submit this request as one of your subusers if you include their ID in the `on-behalf-of` header in the request.
data.reasoning: Description is extremely vague about what validation entails and why it's important. Missing context about DNS verification, validation requirements, and the relationship to email delivery and subuser association. Enhanced description explains the validation process, its purpose in the Link Branding workflow, and what the response contains.

data.current_summary: Associate a branded link with a subuser
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to associate a branded link with a subuser account.**

Link branding can be associated with subusers from the parent account. This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's link branding. To associate link branding, the parent account must first create a branded link and validate it. The parent may then associate that branded link with a subuser via the API or the [Subuser Management page of the Twilio SendGrid App](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/subusers).
data.description_suggestion: Associates a validated branded link with a subuser account, enabling the subuser to use the parent account's link branding for email delivery. Requires the branded link to be previously created and validated by the parent account. Accepts the subuser ID in the request body. Returns the association confirmation with the subuser and link branding details. Only parent accounts can associate branded links with their subusers.
data.reasoning: Description explains the workflow context well but lacks specific details about the request body structure (subuser ID parameter) and response format. Enhanced description clarifies the required input (subuser ID), response content, and permission model (parent account only) for better AI agent understanding of prerequisites and expected outcomes.
